query_result.reasoning: Now I have the definitions and caveats for both MAU and daily posts. Let me also retrieve the more detailed discussion from the filing, particularly any additional context about these caveats and their implications.

tool_calls.name: retrieve_information
tool_calls.args: {"prompt": "What additional discussion exists in the filing about AI-generated content, AI-managed accounts, bots, or the reliability of engagement metrics? Look for any disclosure about how AI may affect MAU, daily posts, or other user engagement metrics in {{spacex_s1}}."}

parsed_args.prompt: What additional discussion exists in the filing about AI-generated content, AI-managed accounts, bots, or the reliability of engagement metrics? Look for any disclosure about how AI may affect MAU, daily posts, or other user engagement metrics in {{spacex_s1}}.

tool_call.name: retrieve_information

parsed_args.prompt: What additional discussion exists in the filing about AI-generated content, AI-managed accounts, bots, or the reliability of engagement metrics? Look for any disclosure about how AI may affect MAU, daily posts, or other user engagement metrics in {{spacex_s1}}.

tool_output.output:
The prospectus summary’s disclosure about engagement metrics includes a clear acknowledgment that **AI-generated content and AI-managed accounts may be included in the daily posts metric**, though it does not provide comparable disclosure for MAU or the reliability of those measures. The relevant language appears in the definition of “daily posts” within the chunk that outlines key terms:

> “ **‘daily posts’ on X and Grok** refers to the aggregate volume of original posts, replies, reposts, quotes and media shared daily by users on the X platform, and the real-time interactions, analysis and generative capabilities provided to a user by Grok. **This may include posts generated by AI or accounts managed by AI.** ”  
> (From the fragment beginning with *“to deliver consumer and enterprise applications…”*)

This disclosure indicates that the daily posts metric is not limited to human-generated content; it can be affected by AI-synthesized material and by accounts controlled by AI (which would encompass bots). No similar carve-out or warning is provided for the MAU figure in any of the chunks you supplied. The MAU discussion only states that monthly active users “provide an estimated measure of the size and engagement of our user base” and emphasizes that management prioritizes revenue and operating margin over MAU growth. The filing does not explicitly discuss the reliability of engagement metrics in connection with AI influence beyond this one note in the definition of daily posts.
